text.title: PM Framework Focus Areas Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the PM Framework Focus Areas methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: ADR MUST list practices for ALL five focus areas (Initiating / Planning / Executing / M&C / Closing); partial ADRs are rejected.
rule.rationale: missing areas hide unowned work; the framework's whole point is full lifecycle coverage.

rule.statement: Each area MUST list >=1 practice in use (e.g. 'Sprint Planning' for Executing) — abstract bullet points without named practices are rejected.
rule.rationale: abstract bullets don't drive behaviour; named practices do.

rule.statement: Each named practice MUST cite evidence (artefact in repo, cadence on calendar, ticket label); un-anchored practices are rejected.
rule.rationale: agent-listed practices without evidence reflect aspiration, not reality.

rule.statement: ADR MUST name a single decision_owner; ownerless ADRs decay.
rule.rationale: an ADR without a named owner is read-only and never revisited.

rule.statement: ADR MUST set a review cadence (monthly / quarterly) and the next review_due date.
rule.rationale: without a review cadence the ADR drifts from reality and stops being a source of truth.
